parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces a retry/backoff system (retry.mjs, 203 new lines), BigModel error dispatch (bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 new lines), and deletes process.mjs entirely — yet ships without v0.4.6 UAT evidence and without visible idempotency guards on retried API calls. The highest-risk surface (retry × glm-client × tracked-jobs) has unit tests but no integration or UAT validation under real failure conditions.

findings.title: Deleted scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) with no visible migration test
findings.body: The entire process.mjs module was removed (-67 lines). Any remaining import of this module will throw a runtime Cannot find module error. The test suite has no 'process.test.mjs' file, and none of the existing test filenames (args, bigmodel-errors, fs, job-render, etc.) suggest coverage for process-management edge cases that this module may have handled (signal trapping, exit handling, child-process cleanup). The deletion in the same bundle as a retry refactor means a crash inside a retry loop could orphan child processes or miss cleanup.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for 'process.mjs' imports. If any caller remains, this is a shipping blocker. Add a dedicated test that validates signal/exit behavior still works after the refactor, or document that the functionality was intentionally dropped and why.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 retry/backoff feature — highest-risk change unvalidated
findings.body: UAT reports exist for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 (test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.{4,5}/) but NOT for v0.4.6. The v0.4.6 commit message explicitly calls out 'BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff' as the core feature. Retry logic is the single most dangerous category of change: it amplifies partial failures, can duplicate side effects, and behaves differently under real network conditions vs. mocked unit tests. The retry.test.mjs (352 lines) covers unit-level behavior but cannot validate real API interaction with backoff, rate-limit headers, or idempotency.
findings.recommendation: Block ship until a v0.4.6 UAT run is produced that exercises: (1) a real API call that fails and is retried, (2) a call that partially succeeds (e.g., 200 but malformed body), (3) a call that exhausts retries, (4) a concurrent retry scenario. Minimally, extend the v0.4.5 UAT harness with retry-specific scenarios.

findings.title: Retry module (203 lines) has no visible idempotency mechanism for non-GET API calls
findings.body: retry.mjs is 203 new lines of retry logic that integrates with glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed). If the BigModel API calls are POST/PUT (e.g., job submission), a retry on a network timeout could duplicate the operation if the server already processed it. The diff stat shows no new idempotency-key header, no request deduplication, and no conditional-retry based on HTTP method. Under real conditions (transient 503 followed by success), this silently creates duplicate jobs or submissions.
findings.recommendation: Audit every call site where retry() wraps a non-GET request. Add either: (a) an idempotency key header, (b) method-aware retry that only retries GET/HEAD by default, or (c) a documented opt-in with a comment explaining why the operation is safe to retry. This is a latent data-integrity bug even if current usage happens to be safe.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) + retry.mjs interaction can corrupt job state on partial retry
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs was heavily modified alongside the retry addition. If a job is tracked as 'in-progress', then the API call fails, and retry creates a new attempt, the job tracking state may become inconsistent: the original attempt is still marked 'in-progress' while the retry writes a new entry or overwrites it. The diff stat shows no test file specifically named for tracked-jobs integration with retry. job-render.test.mjs (144 lines) likely tests rendering, not state consistency under retry.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test that: (1) starts a tracked job, (2) simulates a failure mid-flight, (3) triggers retry, (4) asserts the job state is consistent (single entry, correct final status). Without this, a user hitting transient failures will accumulate zombie 'in-progress' entries that never resolve.

next_steps: Run `grep -r 'process.mjs' scripts/ commands/ hooks/` to confirm no dangling imports from the deleted module.
Execute a v0.4.6 UAT run covering retry scenarios before promoting past v0.4.6.
Audit all retry() call sites for HTTP method safety — confirm none wrap non-idempotent operations without guards.
Add a tracked-jobs + retry integration test that validates state consistency after a failed-then-retried job.
Verify that the retry backoff parameters (delay, max attempts) are configurable and not hardcoded to values that could trigger rate limits.
